title: Review: fix P0-P3 review findings from fifth round
notes:
Maintainer risk: LOW — two correctness bugs in the JSON number/string skip functions, both in the same hand-rolled parser. No security exposure (secrets are not logged, key material zeroized). The bugs affect edge-case inputs unlikely in real secrets stores but technically wrong per RFC 8259.
Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) did not audit the AWS/GCP/Azure backend error mapping exhaustively — many service error codes exist and the mapping is a judgment call; (2) did not check whether rsa::pss::SigningKey<Sha256> implements ZeroizeOnDrop in the workspace-pinned version. Would raise confidence if: cargo test --all-features passes with the two JSON parser fixes applied, and a quick grep of the rsa crate source confirms ZeroizeOnDrop on SigningKey.
